H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (Pebble, n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) is HEALING's largest evidence base with 66% positive outcomes. Qualitative data shows 10/29 'Loved it' and 10/29 'Liked it' music reception, settle_time at 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon underpredicts overstimulation zone at 6.859 (-0.29 delta) — largest negative model error in HEALING. YouTube: 30 views (highest in HEALING) but NULL retention. Distribution slightly right-skewed with modes at 7-9.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Highly effective for overstimulation with strong listener approval, yet remains largely undiscovered despite being the most-viewed HEALING protocol.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (Rainfall, n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) shows 65% positive with qualitative 'Neutral' music cluster (6/21). Settle_time bimodal: 10 minutes (10/21) and immediate (3/21). AutoGluon prediction aligns (~6.9). YouTube views: 5. Distribution symmetric around 7.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Balanced overstimulation relief showing typical early entrainment patterns. Mixed music reactions suggest room for groove refinement.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (Glacier, n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) maintains overstimulation consistency (64% positive). Qualitative: 10/23 'Very settling' and 10/23 'Balanced' rhythm opinions. AutoGluon does not flag. YouTube views: 8. Distribution tight around 7-8 with no scores below 3.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Reliable overstimulation support with clear settling rhythm feedback. Consistent but not exceptional.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (Meadow, n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) demonstrates the strongest anxiety-descent signal in HEALING with 63% positive outcomes. Qualitative data reveals 10/23 listeners reporting moderate improvement and 8/23 noticeable improvement, with settle_time clustering at 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts anxiety-zone score of 7.838 (+0.62 delta), suggesting model expects higher efficacy than observed — this protocol also appears as cross-bucket influence for low-intensity zones. YouTube reach remains minimal (7 views, NULL retention), indicating undiscovered potential. Distribution shows mild right-skew with modes at 7-8 but tail extending to 10.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: This anxiety-relief protocol helps most listeners feel noticeably calmer, especially during stressful moments or therapy sessions, though it hasn't reached many people yet.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (Cottage, n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) shows tighter variance than L1 with 67% positive outcomes. Qualitative feedback includes 6/20 'Neutral' music opinions and bimodal settle_time (5 vs 20 minutes), suggesting subgroup entrainment differences. AutoGluon prediction aligns near observed mean (~7.5). YouTube views at 6 with NULL retention. Distribution is more symmetric than L1 with concentration at 7-8 range.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: A solid anxiety protocol with mixed music reactions — some find it soothing, others neutral. Works well for about two-thirds of listeners.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (Valley, n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) maintains HEALING anxiety consistency with 61% positive rate. Qualitative data shows 2/20 'No effect' reports and settle_time clustering at 10 minutes with 'near the end' tail (4/20). AutoGluon predicts ~7.6. YouTube views at 4. Distribution shows slight bimodality with secondary mass at 9-10.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Consistent calming effects reported, particularly when used for full sessions. A few listeners noticed no change.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (Anchor, n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) aligns with crisis-protocol expectation of lower means (53% positive). Qualitative data paradoxically shows 8/24 'Significant improvement' and 7/24 'Noticeable improvement' despite lower aggregate score — consistent with high-baseline-distress listeners. AutoGluon predicts crisis-zone 7.955 for H-CR-L2 not L1, suggesting design mismatch. YouTube views: 6. Distribution centered at 6-8 with no scores above 9.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Designed for crisis moments — over half report meaningful improvement but the average stays modest, as expected when listeners arrive in extreme distress.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (Harbor, n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) shows 55% positive with qualitative split between 'Neutral' (6) and 'Loved it' (5) music opinions. AutoGluon identifies this as top crisis prediction (7.955) and cross-bucket for low-intensity zones — largest positive delta in crisis family (+1.44). YouTube views: 4. Distribution similar to L1 with slight rightward shift.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Similar crisis tool with immediate calming as a key strength. The model suggests it might help beyond crisis situations too.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (Horizon, n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) matches L2 mean with higher variance. Notable qualitative signal: 7/19 report 'Immediate' settling (within 1-2 minutes) — fastest in crisis family. Music opinions more mixed (7 'Liked', 4 'Not liked'). AutoGluon does not flag. YouTube views: 5. Distribution shows mass at 7-8 with tail to 10.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Settles listeners fastest in the crisis series, but outcomes remain modest. Some listeners didn't connect with the music.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (Lantern, n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) leads sleep protocols with 65% positive. Qualitative: 13/15 'Myself' listeners, settle_time at 5 minutes (5/15) and immediate (3/15). AutoGluon predicts sleep-zone 7.539 (+0.35 delta) and cross-bucks to low-intensity. Notable embedding drift (sim=0.530). YouTube views: 4. Distribution shows secondary mode at 9-10.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Sleep support that exceeds expectations with rapid settling, especially during therapy sessions. Minimal discoverability so far.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (Hammock, n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) has tightest variance in sleep family (67% positive). Qualitative reveals 6/15 'A client/patient' listeners and 2/15 'No effect' — only sleep protocol with null reports. Settle_time longer: 10 minutes (7/15) and 20 minutes (3/15). YouTube views: 5. Distribution concentrated at 7-8.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed effectiveness reported — works well for sleep but some caregivers using it for clients saw no effect. Longer settle times than L1.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (Midnight, n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) shows 64% positive with 5/22 'Significant improvement' — highest in sleep family. Qualitative: 7/22 'A client/patient', settle_time 5-10 minutes. YouTube views: 3. Distribution similar to L1 with right tail.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Strong sleep protocol with consistent improvement reports and notable 'significant improvement' cluster among users.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (Compass, n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) lowest Focus mean (56% positive). Qualitative: 9/23 'Slightly busy' rhythm opinion — only protocol with notable 'busy' signal. AutoGluon predicts focus-zone 7.728 for T-FC-L3 not L1 (+1.27 delta). YouTube views: 5. Distribution broad with left tail to 3.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus aid with notable 'busy rhythm' friction reported by listeners. Underperforms compared to its siblings in the Focus family.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint, n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) substantial improvement over L1 (66% positive). Qualitative: 6/17 'Neutral' music but 5/17 'Loved it', settle_time 10 minutes (9/17). AutoGluon aligns better than for L1. YouTube views: 4. Distribution centered at 7-8 with right tail.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Exceeds model predictions for focus clarity with strong 'loved it' reactions. Deserves wider testing and discovery.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (Summit, n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) highest Focus mean (71% positive) with notable variance. Qualitative: 11/29 'Significant improvement', 15/29 settle at 10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts focus-zone best (7.728, +0.10 delta) and cross-bucket to low-intensity. Highest YouTube views in THRIVING (15) but NULL retention. Embedding drift high (sim=0.532). Distribution bimodal with peaks at 7 and 10.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Top-performing focus protocol with high improvement variability — works exceptionally well for some, less for others. Gaining YouTube traction.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (Ember, n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) shows 53% positive — lowest in Creative Flow. Qualitative: 8/21 'Slight improvement', immediate settle (6/21). AutoGluon predicts higher creative-zone potential. YouTube views: 2. Distribution broad with mass at 6-7.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Early-stage creative flow protocol with minimal listener exposure. Most report slight to moderate improvement with immediate settling.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (Current, n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) tighter variance than L1 (58% positive). Qualitative: 5/15 'Significant improvement', settle_time 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts mindclarity-zone 7.538 (+0.75 delta). YouTube views: 2. Distribution narrow at 6-8.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Creative support showing narrower, more predictable effects than L1. A few report significant breakthroughs.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (Aurora, n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) highest Creative Flow mean with 67% positive and no scores below 7. Qualitative: 7/25 'Noticeable', 6/25 'Significant', settle_time 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon does not flag. YouTube views: 7 (highest in THRIVING). Distribution remarkably tight — developing confidence due to n<20 only.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Best creative flow outcomes in THRIVING with remarkably consistent scores — no ratings below 7. Small audience but strong signal.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (Stone, n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) tightest Maintenance variance (64% positive). Qualitative: 9/23 settle at 20 minutes — 39% exceed protocol length. 4/19 'Did NOT settle'. AutoGluon predicts maintenance best for T-MB-L3 not L1. YouTube views: 3. Distribution narrow at 7-8.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Balanced maintenance protocol but many listeners need more time to settle than the design allows. Unique 'did not settle' reports.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (Axis, n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) widest Maintenance variance (59% positive). Qualitative: 7/16 'A client/patient', settle_time 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts maintenance best for L3. YouTube views: 4. Distribution shows bimodal tendency with spread 4-9.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: High variance suggests population split — works extremely well for some, poorly for others. Caregiver-heavy listener base.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (Orbit, n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) 71% positive but developing confidence. Qualitative: 4/19 'Did NOT settle', 7/19 'Liked it'. AutoGluon predicts maintenance-zone best (7.693, +0.76 delta). YouTube views: 3. Distribution shows secondary mass at 9-10.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Maintenance protocol showing developing stability patterns with strong positive music reception but concerning non-settle rate.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (Breeze, n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) Mood & Energy ASCENT with 54% positive. Qualitative: 10/22 'Loved it' music (highest in TRANSFORMING), settle_time 20 minutes (6/22). AutoGluon predicts low-intensity-zone best (8.230, -1.49 delta) — largest negative delta in library. YouTube views: 8. Cross-bucket routing to X-MD-L1, X-MD-L2. Distribution slightly right-skewed.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Energy protocol with strongest qualitative praise ('loved it' density) but largest model underperformance — predicted 8.2, got 6.7.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise, n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) 64% positive. Qualitative: 7/15 'Significant improvement', 6/15 'Loved it' music. Settle_time bimodal: 20 minutes (4/15) and immediate (4/15). AutoGluon cross-buckles to low-intensity. YouTube views: 8. Embedding drift notable (sim=0.532). Distribution centered at 7.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Mood elevation showing significant improvement clusters. Bimodal settle times suggest two distinct listener journeys.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (Updraft, n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) 56% positive with 3/25 'No effect'. Qualitative: 5/25 'Doing chores' activity, settle_time 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts low-intensity but observed lower. YouTube views: 4. Distribution shows left tail to 3.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Lowest scoring mood protocol but aligns with low-affect state design. Some report no effect, most report noticeable improvement.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (Footpath, n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) Motivation ASCENT with 45% positive — lowest in TRANSFORMING. Qualitative: 6/17 'Noticeable', 5/17 'Significant' despite low aggregate. AutoGluon predicts motivation-zone best (7.876, +1.52 delta) and cross-bucket to low-intensity — largest positive delta in library. YouTube views: 5. Settle_time fast: 5 minutes (9/17). Distribution broad with left tail.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation protocol with strong improvement reports but low average score. The model thinks it should work much better — possible ascending-curve mismatch.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (Trail, n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) 43% positive. Qualitative: 6/21 'Noticeable', 5/21 'Slight', settle_time 10 minutes (5/21) and 20 minutes (5/21). AutoGluon cross-buckles to low-intensity. YouTube views: 7. Distribution narrower than L1 but left-shifted.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed motivational effects, particularly during nighttime use. Longer settle times suggest the ascent may be too gradual for some.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (Switchback, n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) highest Motivation mean (62% positive) but second-highest variance in library. Qualitative: 8/17 'Moderate', 1/17 'Did NOT settle'. AutoGluon does not flag but shows highest embedding drift (sim=0.507). YouTube views: 7. Distribution bimodal with masses at 4 and 9-10.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Highest improvement volatility in TRANSFORMING — dramatic split between low and high responders. Analytical framing unstable across runs.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (Cavern, n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) leads Mind & Clarity with 54% positive. Qualitative: 2/25 'It made things worse' — only TRANSFORMING protocol with harm signal. Music: 9/25 'Loved it', 9/25 'Liked it'. AutoGluon predicts mindclarity best for L2. YouTube views: 8. Distribution centered at 7 with slight left tail.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Clarity protocol with higher experience ratings than the score suggests. A rare 'made things worse' signal warrants attention.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (Starlight, n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) aligns with L1 (56% positive). Qualitative: 7/20 'Significant improvement', 6/20 'Loved it' music. Settle_time bimodal: immediate (6/20) and 10 minutes (6/20). AutoGluon predicts mindclarity-zone best (7.538, +0.73 delta). YouTube views: 12. Distribution similar to L1.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Surprisingly consistent clarity for a developing signal. Strong 'loved it' reactions and immediate settling for half the listeners.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) lowest mean in library with highest variance (50% positive). Qualitative: 2/17 'It made things worse', 4/17 'Too busy' rhythm. Distribution clearly bimodal: masses at 3-4 and 8-9 with gap at 5-7. AutoGluon does not flag. YouTube views: 8. Embedding drift notable (sim=0.696). Panel converged: population mismatch or groove failure.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Contentious protocol with major split in experience — some find it helpful, others actively unsettling. Requires urgent investigation.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The data suggests a coherent efficacy gradient across the three series with HEALING and THRIVING converging near 7.0 mean scores while TRANSFORMING trails at 6.59, consistent with its ascending-arc design for low-affect states.
• HEALING protocols show the strongest consistency (std 1.77) and largest evidence base (n=574), supporting their regulatory function.
• THRIVING matches HEALING's mean (6.96) with tighter spread (std 1.72), reflecting optimization intent through HOLD-type stabilization.
• TRANSFORMING exhibits 23% greater variance than HEALING (std 1.87) and lower average outcomes, structurally expected for population entering in diverse low-affect states.
• AutoGluon feature importance ranks engagement metrics (avg_view_percentage 0.277, avg_view_duration 0.221) above protocol design parameters, suggesting listener exposure quality may mediate efficacy more than Hz architecture alone.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series efficacy profiles align with design intent but reveal nuanced boundary-crossing effects.
• HEALING DESCENT protocols cluster tightly around 7.0-7.2 mean with 61-67% positive outcomes, supporting gradual entrainment for regulation.
• THRIVING HOLD protocols diverge within category — Focus & Clarity L3 (7.63) substantially outperforms L1 (6.46), while Maintenance protocols show wider spread (6.68-7.04).
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols (Motivation & Drive, Mood & Energy) underperform AutoGluon predictions by 0.5-1.5 points, with X-ME-L1 showing largest delta (predicted 8.23 vs observed 6.74).
• Cross-bucket routing anomalies persist: AutoGluon routes crisis to H-CR-L2 (7.96), low-affect to X-ME-L1 (8.23), and focus to T-FC-L3 (7.73) — all exceeding their design zones.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols exhibit distributional or predictive anomalies warranting targeted investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45) shows bimodal distribution with 50% positive rate — highest variance in library, suggesting population mismatch or groove-style failure.
• X-MD-L3 (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38) carries second-highest variance with developing confidence — qualitative 'Did NOT settle' reports (4/19) contradict its high mean.
• T-FC-L3 (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97) outperforms AutoGluon prediction (7.73) while exhibiting notable embedding drift (sim=0.532) — efficacy signal may be evolving.
• H-OS-L1 (n=95, mean=7.15) exceeds AutoGluon prediction (6.86) by 0.29 points — largest positive delta in HEALING series, suggesting model underestimates overstimulation efficacy.
• X-ME-L1 (n=39, mean=6.74) underperforms AutoGluon low-intensity prediction (8.23) by 1.49 points — largest negative delta in library, indicating potential ascending-arc miscalibration.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Convergent signals point to specific protocol refinements and architectural investigations.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.70): Investigate groove-style mismatch and subgroup effects — bimodal distribution with 50% positive rate suggests population split; consider L3 redesign with steeper clarity curve before scaling.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-OS-L1 (n=95, mean=7.15): AutoGluon underpredicts by 0.29 despite strongest HEALING evidence base; audit feature weighting for overstimulation zone and test discovery interventions (YouTube: 30 views, NULL retention).
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 (n=35, mean=7.63): Highest Focus efficacy with cross-bucket routing to low-intensity zones; develop L2 variant to test parameter transferability and monitor embedding drift (sim=0.532).
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L1 (n=39, mean=6.74): Largest AutoGluon negative delta (-1.49) in low-intensity routing; adjust ASCENT curve steepness or extend hold phase to improve entrainment before ascent.
• X-MD-L1 (n=22, mean=6.36): Motivation protocol underperforms prediction (7.88) by 1.52 points; test segmented routing by initial motivation level per qualitative 'Slight improvement' cluster.
• T-MB-L1 (n=25, mean=7.04): 36% report 20+ minute settle times vs 15-minute design; extend hold phase or add L2 variant with slower ease-out.
• H-CR-L2 (n=33, mean=6.52): AutoGluon predicts crisis-zone best (7.96) and cross-bucket for low-intensity; validate as dual-purpose protocol with advisers routing acute and low-affect listeners.
• T-CF-L3 (n=18, mean=7.28): Highest Creative Flow score with developing confidence; create L2 variant with slightly faster pacing to test amplification potential.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Semantic stability analysis reveals concerning drift in key protocols and clustering that transcends series boundaries.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 26 of 30 protocols show cosine similarity <0.85 between earliest and latest runs — X-MD-L3 (0.507), H-SL-L1 (0.530), T-FC-L3 (0.532), X-ME-L2 (0.532) indicate substantial analytical reinterpretation, likely driven by accumulating qualitative data and small-n instability.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Strongest pairs are T-FC-L1↔T-FC-L2 (0.878) and H-SL-L1↔H-SL-L2 (0.869), confirming design-consistent framing within Focus and Sleep families; H-OS-L3 is most analytically isolated (mean_sim=0.677), suggesting unique acoustic or contextual features.
• Confidence note: Drift analysis spans only 79 days (2026-04-02 to 2026-06-20) with single-volume data — insufficient for longitudinal stability claims; treat drift signals as hypothesis-generating only.

Distribution concentrated at 7-8.

by_protocol.T-CF-L1: T-CF-L1 (Ember, n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) shows 53% positive — lowest in Creative Flow. Qualitative: 8/21 'Slight improvement', immediate settle (6/21). AutoGluon predicts higher creative-zone potential. YouTube views: 2. Distribution broad with mass at 6-7.
by_protocol.T-CF-L2: T-CF-L2 (Current, n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) tighter variance than L1 (58% positive). Qualitative: 5/15 'Significant improvement', settle_time 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts mindclarity-zone 7.538 (+0.75 delta). YouTube views: 2. Distribution narrow at 6-8.
by_protocol.T-CF-L3: T-CF-L3 (Aurora, n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) highest Creative Flow mean with 67% positive and no scores below 7. Qualitative: 7/25 'Noticeable', 6/25 'Significant', settle_time 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon does not flag. YouTube views: 7 (highest in THRIVING). Distribution remarkably tight — developing confidence due to n<20 only.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1: T-FC-L1 (Compass, n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) lowest Focus mean (56% positive). Qualitative: 9/23 'Slightly busy' rhythm opinion — only protocol with notable 'busy' signal. AutoGluon predicts focus-zone 7.728 for T-FC-L3 not L1 (+1.27 delta). YouTube views: 5. Distribution broad with left tail to 3.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint, n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) substantial improvement over L1 (66% positive). Qualitative: 6/17 'Neutral' music but 5/17 'Loved it', settle_time 10 minutes (9/17). AutoGluon aligns better than for L1. YouTube views: 4. Distribution centered at 7-8 with right tail.
by_protocol.T-FC-L3: T-FC-L3 (Summit, n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) highest Focus mean (71% positive) with notable variance. Qualitative: 11/29 'Significant improvement', 15/29 settle at 10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts focus-zone best (7.728, +0.10 delta) and cross-bucket to low-intensity. Highest YouTube views in THRIVING (15) but NULL retention. Embedding drift high (sim=0.532). Distribution bimodal with peaks at 7 and 10.
by_protocol.T-MB-L1: T-MB-L1 (Stone, n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) tightest Maintenance variance (64% positive). Qualitative: 9/23 settle at 20 minutes — 39% exceed protocol length. 4/19 'Did NOT settle'. AutoGluon predicts maintenance best for T-MB-L3 not L1. YouTube views: 3. Distribution narrow at 7-8.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: T-MB-L2 (Axis, n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) widest Maintenance variance (59% positive). Qualitative: 7/16 'A client/patient', settle_time 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts maintenance best for L3. YouTube views: 4. Distribution shows bimodal tendency with spread 4-9.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: T-MB-L3 (Orbit, n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) 71% positive but developing confidence. Qualitative: 4/19 'Did NOT settle', 7/19 'Liked it'. AutoGluon predicts maintenance-zone best (7.693, +0.76 delta). YouTube views: 3. Distribution shows secondary mass at 9-10.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: X-MC-L1 (Cavern, n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) leads Mind & Clarity with 54% positive. Qualitative: 2/25 'It made things worse' — only TRANSFORMING protocol with harm signal. Music: 9/25 'Loved it', 9/25 'Liked it'. AutoGluon predicts mindclarity best for L2. YouTube views: 8. Distribution centered at 7 with slight left tail.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: X-MC-L2 (Starlight, n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) aligns with L1 (56% positive). Qualitative: 7/20 'Significant improvement', 6/20 'Loved it' music. Settle_time bimodal: immediate (6/20) and 10 minutes (6/20). AutoGluon predicts mindclarity-zone best (7.538, +0.73 delta). YouTube views: 12. Distribution similar to L1.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) lowest mean in library with highest variance (50% positive). Qualitative: 2/17 'It made things worse', 4/17 'Too busy' rhythm. Distribution clearly bimodal: masses at 3-4 and 8-9 with gap at 5-7. AutoGluon does not flag. YouTube views: 8. Embedding drift notable (sim=0.696). Panel converged: population mismatch or groove failure.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: X-MD-L1 (Footpath, n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) Motivation ASCENT with 45% positive — lowest in TRANSFORMING. Qualitative: 6/17 'Noticeable', 5/17 'Significant' despite low aggregate. AutoGluon predicts motivation-zone best (7.876, +1.52 delta) and cross-bucket to low-intensity — largest positive delta in library. YouTube views: 5. Settle_time fast: 5 minutes (9/17). Distribution broad with left tail.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: X-MD-L2 (Trail, n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) 43% positive. Qualitative: 6/21 'Noticeable', 5/21 'Slight', settle_time 10 minutes (5/21) and 20 minutes (5/21). AutoGluon cross-buckles to low-intensity. YouTube views: 7. Distribution narrower than L1 but left-shifted.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: X-MD-L3 (Switchback, n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) highest Motivation mean (62% positive) but second-highest variance in library. Qualitative: 8/17 'Moderate', 1/17 'Did NOT settle'. AutoGluon does not flag but shows highest embedding drift (sim=0.507). YouTube views: 7. Distribution bimodal with masses at 4 and 9-10.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X-ME-L1 (Breeze, n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) Mood & Energy ASCENT with 54% positive. Qualitative: 10/22 'Loved it' music (highest in TRANSFORMING), settle_time 20 minutes (6/22). AutoGluon predicts low-intensity-zone best (8.230, -1.49 delta) — largest negative delta in library. YouTube views: 8. Cross-bucket routing to X-MD-L1, X-MD-L2. Distribution slightly right-skewed.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise, n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) 64% positive. Qualitative: 7/15 'Significant improvement', 6/15 'Loved it' music. Settle_time bimodal: 20 minutes (4/15) and immediate (4/15). AutoGluon cross-buckles to low-intensity. YouTube views: 8. Embedding drift notable (sim=0.532). Distribution centered at 7.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 (Updraft, n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) 56% positive with 3/25 'No effect'. Qualitative: 5/25 'Doing chores' activity, settle_time 5-10 minutes. AutoGluon predicts low-intensity but observed lower. YouTube views: 4. Distribution shows left tail to 3.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: This anxiety-relief protocol helps most listeners feel noticeably calmer, especially during stressful moments or therapy sessions, though it hasn't reached many people yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: A solid anxiety protocol with mixed music reactions — some find it soothing, others neutral. Works well for about two-thirds of listeners.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Consistent calming effects reported, particularly when used for full sessions. A few listeners noticed no change.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Designed for crisis moments — over half report meaningful improvement but the average stays modest, as expected when listeners arrive in extreme distress.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Similar crisis tool with immediate calming as a key strength. The model suggests it might help beyond crisis situations too.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Settles listeners fastest in the crisis series, but outcomes remain modest. Some listeners didn't connect with the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Highly effective for overstimulation with strong listener approval, yet remains largely undiscovered despite being the most-viewed HEALING protocol.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Balanced overstimulation relief showing typical early entrainment patterns. Mixed music reactions suggest room for groove refinement.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Reliable overstimulation support with clear settling rhythm feedback. Consistent but not exceptional.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Sleep support that exceeds expectations with rapid settling, especially during therapy sessions. Minimal discoverability so far.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Mixed effectiveness reported — works well for sleep but some caregivers using it for clients saw no effect. Longer settle times than L1.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Strong sleep protocol with consistent improvement reports and notable 'significant improvement' cluster among users.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Early-stage creative flow protocol with minimal listener exposure. Most report slight to moderate improvement with immediate settling.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Creative support showing narrower, more predictable effects than L1. A few report significant breakthroughs.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Best creative flow outcomes in THRIVING with remarkably consistent scores — no ratings below 7. Small audience but strong signal.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus aid with notable 'busy rhythm' friction reported by listeners. Underperforms compared to its siblings in the Focus family.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Exceeds model predictions for focus clarity with strong 'loved it' reactions. Deserves wider testing and discovery.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Top-performing focus protocol with high improvement variability — works exceptionally well for some, less for others. Gaining YouTube traction.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Balanced maintenance protocol but many listeners need more time to settle than the design allows. Unique 'did not settle' reports.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: High variance suggests population split — works extremely well for some, poorly for others. Caregiver-heavy listener base.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Maintenance protocol showing developing stability patterns with strong positive music reception but concerning non-settle rate.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Clarity protocol with higher experience ratings than the score suggests. A rare 'made things worse' signal warrants attention.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Surprisingly consistent clarity for a developing signal. Strong 'loved it' reactions and immediate settling for half the listeners.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Contentious protocol with major split in experience — some find it helpful, others actively unsettling. Requires urgent investigation.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation protocol with strong improvement reports but low average score. The model thinks it should work much better — possible ascending-curve mismatch.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Mixed motivational effects, particularly during nighttime use. Longer settle times suggest the ascent may be too gradual for some.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Highest improvement volatility in TRANSFORMING — dramatic split between low and high responders. Analytical framing unstable across runs.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Energy protocol with strongest qualitative praise ('loved it' density) but largest model underperformance — predicted 8.2, got 6.7.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Mood elevation showing significant improvement clusters. Bimodal settle times suggest two distinct listener journeys.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Lowest scoring mood protocol but aligns with low-affect state design. Some report no effect, most report noticeable improvement.
